entries.definition:
ʿ–w–n herd of zebras; to be in a middle state, being
middle-aged; tall palm tree; helper, backer, to assist. Of this root,
five forms occur 11 times in the Qur’an: أَعَانَ ʾaʿāna twice;
تَعَاوَن taʿāwan twice; نَسْتَعِين nastaʿīn four times; مُسْتَعَان mustaʿān twice
and عَوَان ʿawān once.

أَعَانَ ʾaʿāna [v. IV trans.] to assist (25:4) وَأَعَانَهُ عَلَيْهِ قَوْمٌ آخَرُونَ
and other people helped him with it.

تَعَاوَن taʿāwan [imper. of v. VI intrans.] help one another,
cooperate with one another (5:2) وَتَعَاوَنُوا عَلَى الْبِرِّ وَالتَّقْوَى and assist
each other in charity and pious actions.

نَسْتَعِين nastaʿīn [imperf. of v. X intrans.] to seek assistance
(2:153) اسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ وَالصَّلَاةِ seek help through steadfastness and
prayer.

مُسْتَعَان mustaʿān [pass. part.] one whose assistance is sought,
one who is beseeched (12:18) وَاللَّهُ الْمُسْتَعَانُ عَلَى مَا تَصِفُونَ and it is
God whose help is sought in [overcoming] what you describe.

عَوَان ʿawān [quasi-act. part.] (of an animal) neither young nor
old, middle-aged (2:68) إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَا فَارِضٌ وَلَا بِكْرٌ عَوَانٌ بَيْنَ ذَلِكَ
it is a cow [which should be] neither too old nor too young, but in-
between.

ماعون māʿūn (see م/ع/ن m–ʿ–n).
